summary: Today's eDiscovery newsletter covers IoT forensics best practices, AI verification requirements for lawyers, market intelligence on eDiscovery software growth, CISA's critical infrastructure guidance, and insights from high-profile eDiscovery cases.

stories.headline: Spot the Spoof in this Post About IoT Forensics: Forensics Best Practices
stories.author: Doug Austin
stories.summary: Melinda Redenius at Veracity Forensics provides three Internet of Things (IoT) scenarios – two real, one fake – challenging readers to identify the fabricated scenario. This interactive approach highlights the growing importance of IoT forensics in legal proceedings.
key_points: IoT devices are increasingly relevant in litigation and investigations
Understanding authentic vs. fabricated IoT evidence is crucial for practitioners
Interactive forensics exercises help build practical skills

stories.headline: From Depp-Heard to Epstein: My Interview on the Data Xposure Podcast: eDiscovery Trends
stories.author: Doug Austin
stories.summary: Doug Austin discusses the impact of eDiscovery in high-profile cases ranging from the Depp-Heard trial to the Epstein documents in an interview with Mike Hamilton of Exterro on the Data Xposure podcast.
key_points: High-profile cases demonstrate the critical role of eDiscovery in modern litigation
Podcast interview covers practical insights from major eDiscovery events
Discussion bridges entertainment industry and complex legal document handling

stories.headline: States Are Requiring Lawyers to Verify AI Outputs. Will it Help?: Artificial Intelligence Trends
stories.author: Doug Austin
stories.summary: A growing trend sees states implementing requirements for lawyers to verify AI-generated outputs. The article examines whether these regulations will effectively address hallucinated citations and AI-related errors in legal work.
key_points: Multiple states now require attorney verification of AI outputs
Concerns remain about effectiveness in preventing hallucinated citations
Regulatory landscape evolving to address AI risks in legal practice

stories.headline: CISA's CI Fortify Rewrites the Disconnection Playbook for Critical Infrastructure
stories.author: Complex Discovery OÜ
stories.summary: CISA's new CI Fortify initiative instructs U.S. critical infrastructure operators to plan for weeks or months of disconnected operations. This significantly reshapes vendor-risk diligence and preservation strategy across regulated sectors.
key_points: CI Fortify requires planning for extended disconnected operations
Impacts vendor-risk assessment and preservation obligations
Critical infrastructure operators face new compliance requirements

stories.headline: Market Intelligence: The eDiscovery Software Market from 2025 to 2030
stories.author: Complex Discovery OÜ
stories.summary: Worldwide eDiscovery software spending grows from $6.67 billion in 2025 to a projected $10.95 billion in 2030 at a reconciled 10.41 percent CAGR – the fastest-growing segment in eDiscovery. Cloud-native, AI-enabled platforms continue to drive market expansion.
key_points: eDiscovery software market projected to reach $10.95B by 2030
10.41% CAGR outpaces services growth by 4.66 percentage points
Cloud-native, AI-enabled platforms lead vendor differentiation
AI governance and model transparency emerging as procurement criteria
